title: Account Balance Rollup
summary: Given a list of ledger events, return the final balance per account. Ignore malformed events, but record whether any malformed event was skipped.
required_behavior: Accept a list of events where each event may or may not have the shape {account, delta}.
Apply deltas in order and accumulate balances by account string.
Ignore malformed events without crashing.
Return both the balance map and a boolean indicating whether malformed events were seen.
contract_details: Valid events contain a non-empty account string and an integer delta.
Malformed events do not contribute to balances.
Return shape must always include balances and malformed_seen.
edge_cases: An empty input list returns an empty balance map and malformed_seen=false.
Multiple updates to the same account must accumulate.
Null, missing keys, wrong types, and blank account names are malformed.

title: Threshold Bursts
summary: Given numeric readings and a threshold, return the start and end indexes of contiguous bursts where readings stay strictly above the threshold.
required_behavior: Return a list of inclusive index pairs.
Single-reading bursts are valid.
Readings equal to the threshold are not inside a burst.
contract_details: Indexes are zero-based.
Output order must match the input scan order.
If no bursts exist, return an empty list.
edge_cases: Handle empty input cleanly.
Handle bursts at the beginning or end of the sequence.
Do not merge bursts separated by at least one non-qualifying reading.

title: CSV Split With Quotes
summary: Split a single CSV line into fields, where commas inside double-quoted sections do not split fields and doubled quotes inside quoted fields resolve to a single quote character.
required_behavior: Return a list of parsed field strings.
Treat doubled double-quotes inside a quoted field as an escaped quote.
Preserve empty fields.
contract_details: The input is one line, not a full file.
Leading and trailing spaces are preserved as part of field content.
The parser may assume quotes are balanced.
edge_cases: Handle an empty line as one empty field.
Handle consecutive commas as empty fields.
Handle quoted empty strings.

title: Dependency Batches
summary: Given tasks and dependency edges, return execution batches where each batch can run in parallel. If a cycle exists, return an error result instead of batches.
required_behavior: Each batch contains tasks whose prerequisites were satisfied by earlier batches.
Tasks within a batch must be sorted lexicographically.
If the graph contains a cycle, return an error contract.
contract_details: All tasks mentioned either explicitly or inside edges must appear in the output or error handling.
The success result contains ordered batches.
The error result identifies that a cycle was detected.
edge_cases: Disconnected components are valid.
Duplicate edges should not duplicate work.
An empty task list returns an empty batch list.

title: Inventory Reconcile
summary: Apply inventory operations to a stock map. Restock adds units, reserve removes units only if enough stock exists, and invalid operations must be reported.
required_behavior: Process operations in order.
Return final stock and a list of rejected operation indexes.
Do not let stock counts go negative.
contract_details: Each operation has kind, sku, and qty.
Unknown kinds, invalid quantities, or over-reserve attempts are rejected.
Rejected operations do not mutate stock.
edge_cases: Missing SKUs begin at zero stock.
Zero quantity is invalid.
An entirely invalid operation stream still returns a valid output structure.

title: Session Durations
summary: Given timestamped login/logout events sorted by timestamp, compute the total active duration per user while ignoring unmatched logout events.
required_behavior: Track active login state per user.
A logout without a prior active login is ignored.
If a user is still logged in at the end, do not count an open session.
contract_details: Timestamps are integer seconds and the input is sorted ascending.
Repeated login while already active resets the session start to the new login time.
Return a map from user to total active seconds.
edge_cases: Empty event list returns an empty map.
Multiple users may interleave.
Unmatched final login contributes no duration.

title: Sliding Window Majority
summary: For every contiguous window of size k, return the element that appears strictly more than half the time, or null if no majority exists.
required_behavior: Return one output per valid window in input order.
Use null-like output only when no strict majority exists.
If k is invalid, return an error contract.
contract_details: k must be at least 1 and at most the length of the input list.
A strict majority means count > floor(k / 2).
The success result contains a list named values.
edge_cases: A single-element window always has a majority.
Repeated values may overlap across windows.
An invalid k must not throw.

title: Rule Based Discount
summary: Evaluate an order against ordered discount rules and return the first matching rule plus the discounted total. If no rule matches, return the original total with a no-match marker.
required_behavior: Rules are evaluated in the given order.
Each rule may inspect total, item count, and customer tier.
Return the chosen rule id or a no-match marker.
contract_details: Only the first matching rule applies.
Discount is a percentage integer and must be applied exactly once.
Return both applied_rule and final_total.
edge_cases: An empty rule list should still return a valid no-match output.
A 0% rule is still a valid applied rule.
Customer tier may be null.

title: Merge Touching Intervals
summary: Normalize and merge integer intervals. Intervals that overlap or touch should collapse into one merged interval.
required_behavior: Return merged inclusive intervals sorted by start value.
Treat touching intervals as mergeable.
Ignore malformed interval entries without crashing.
contract_details: Each valid interval has exactly two integer endpoints.
If an interval is reversed, normalize it before merging.
Return a list of [start, end] pairs.
edge_cases: An empty input list returns an empty list.
Single-point intervals are valid.
Malformed entries do not contribute to the result.

title: Top K Frequencies
summary: Given string values and an integer k, return the k most frequent values. Ties are broken lexicographically.
required_behavior: Return values sorted by frequency descending, then lexicographically ascending.
If k exceeds the number of unique values, return all unique values in ranked order.
If k is invalid, return an error contract.
contract_details: Only string values participate in frequency counting.
The success result contains a list named items.
The error result must use reason=invalid_k.
edge_cases: A single-value input is valid.
Repeated ties must still sort deterministically.
k less than 1 is invalid.

title: Queue Wait Times
summary: Simulate a single-server FIFO queue and return the wait time for each valid job.
required_behavior: Process jobs in the input order.
Return one output row per valid job with its wait time.
Ignore malformed jobs without crashing.
contract_details: Each valid job has id, arrive, and duration.
The server is idle when no job has arrived yet.
Return a list of {id, wait} rows in the same order as the valid input jobs.
edge_cases: An empty input list returns an empty list.
Simultaneous arrivals preserve input order.
A zero-duration job is valid.

title: Normalize Phone Book
summary: Normalize contact phone numbers to digits, discard invalid entries, and merge duplicate numbers by collecting unique names.
required_behavior: Strip all non-digit characters from phone strings.
Keep only numbers with exactly 10 digits.
Return phone groups sorted by normalized phone value.
contract_details: Each output row contains phone and names.
Names within a phone group must be unique and lexicographically sorted.
Malformed entries do not appear in the output.
edge_cases: Duplicate names for the same phone should appear once.
Invalid phone lengths are ignored.
An empty input list returns an empty list.

title: Stable Group Runs
summary: Compress consecutive equal string values into run-length records while preserving the scan order.
required_behavior: Return one output row per consecutive run.
Separated runs with the same value must stay separate.
Ignore non-string values without crashing.
contract_details: Each output row contains value and count.
Counts are positive integers.
Return an empty list when no valid string values exist.
edge_cases: A single value yields one run with count 1.
An empty input list yields an empty list.
Non-string values do not merge neighboring runs.

title: Token Bucket Decisions
summary: Simulate a token bucket rate limiter across discrete ticks and return how many requests were allowed at each tick.
required_behavior: Start with a full bucket.
After each tick except the last, refill before the next tick.
Return an error contract for invalid configuration or invalid requests.
contract_details: capacity must be at least 1 and refill must be non-negative.
requests must be a list of non-negative integers.
The success result contains allowed and remaining.
edge_cases: A zero refill rate is valid.
A request may exceed the current token count.
An empty request list returns an empty allowed list.

title: Dense Rankings
summary: Sort score entries descending and assign dense ranks so equal scores share a rank and the next distinct score increments by one.
required_behavior: Sort by score descending, then by name ascending.
Equal scores share the same rank.
Ignore malformed entries without crashing.
contract_details: Each valid input row has name and score.
Each output row contains name and rank.
Return the ranked rows in sorted output order.
edge_cases: An empty input list returns an empty list.
Multiple ties may appear.
A single valid row receives rank 1.

title: Bracket Balance Report
summary: Check whether brackets are balanced and return the index of the first error when they are not.
required_behavior: Support (), [], and {} pairs.
Ignore non-bracket characters.
If the string ends with unmatched open brackets, report the string length as the first error index.
contract_details: A balanced result returns balanced=true and first_error_index=null-like.
An early mismatched or extra closing bracket returns its zero-based index.
Return the same output shape for every input.
edge_cases: An empty string is balanced.
A closing bracket at index 0 is an immediate error.
Nested brackets are valid when properly matched.
